reasoning: The Manifold market stood at 19% on May 29, 2025, despite the fact that the most recent April 2025 CPI print showed headline YoY inflation at 2.3% (below the 2.5% threshold). The low market probability is explained by near-uniform consensus among economists and financial institutions (Vanguard, Capital Economics, Bank of America) that inflation has hit a near-term trough and will re-accelerate aggressively in Q3 and Q4 of 2025 due to the implementation of tariffs under the Trump administration. Estimates for late 2025 CPI consistently sit between 3.5% and 4.0%. Because the market is pricing in the end-of-year or average rate (since it hasn't resolved YES on the April data) and forecasts strongly anticipate an upward trend, the probability of closing 2025 below 2.5% is low. A 15% probability accounts for near-term momentum but respects the overwhelming forecast of a tariff-driven inflation spike.
